search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG106 chronic heart failure monitoring review function fluid status rhythm renal follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic medicine titration, diuretic dose, admission, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Deterioration and fluid status require actual clinician assessment.
No medicine, dose, treatment, admission, or referral decision was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with clinician-established chronic heart failure receiving follow-up.
setting_applicability: Primary, community, outpatient, and specialist follow-up; acute decompensation requires direct urgent care.
UAE_applicability: International NICE evidence requires UAE cardiology, prescribing, laboratory, device, emergency, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG106 last updated 2025-09-03 was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG106 remains current NICE chronic-heart-failure guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE section supports documenting interval symptoms and function, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medicines, renal and hematologic context, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs without generating treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The primary-care review section supports recording updates, clinician medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, care-plan discussion, follow-up, and specialist access without generating a plan.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

unresolved_source_gaps: Heart-failure phenotype and severity, symptoms, weight and fluid trend, rhythm, blood pressure, function, cognition, nutrition, medicines and doses, adherence, laboratory values, ECG, imaging, device status, deterioration, treatment, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
